task_completion.description: Verify that the ARCH (Architecture Design) document was opened in ReqView.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The active document tab or header in the ReqView interface displays 'ARCH' or 'Architecture Design'.

task_completion.description: Verify that a new component named 'Security Audit Module' was created in the ARCH document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A row or item in the ARCH document view clearly displays the text 'Security Audit Module'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the SRS (Software Requirements Specification) document was opened in ReqView.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The active document tab or header in the ReqView interface displays 'SRS' or 'Software Requirements Specification'.

task_completion.description: Verify that a new requirement was created in the SRS document with the specified audit log text.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A row or item in the SRS document view contains the text 'timestamped audit log entry'.

task_completion.description: Verify that a traceability link was established between the new SRS requirement and the ARCH component.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the SRS or ARCH document view, there is a visual link indicator (e.g., a link badge, an ID reference in a traceability column like 'Satisfies' or 'Allocated To', or an arrow) connecting the SRS requirement to the 'Security Audit Module' ARCH component.

task_completion.description: Verify that the project was saved after making the changes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The project name in the ReqView title bar or document tab does not have an unsaved changes indicator (such as an asterisk '*'), or a 'Project Saved' notification is visible.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the ReqView graphical user interface to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the ReqView application window open with visible interactions like mouse clicks, menu navigation, or typing within the application UI.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual ReqView application and not a generic text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface shown is clearly the ReqView application (with its specific toolbars, document views, and link panels), not a code editor like VS Code, nano, or gedit editing raw JSON/XML files.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use terminal commands or scripts to bypass the GUI workflow.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal windows, if visible, do not show commands (like sed, echo, or python scripts) directly modifying the project files in the 'trace_feature_project' directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target ARCH component name is exactly 'Security Audit Module'. The target SRS requirement must contain the text 'timestamped audit log entry'. The project directory is 'trace_feature_project'.
